Oh My Juiceness

July Changes

Your website, rebuilt around the product — fresh oranges, real juice, and the machine that makes it happen.

July 2026

This report walks through everything that changed on ohmyjuiceness.com this month. We started with your ten revision requests, delivered all of them, and then went further — new product photography, an interactive locations map, faster fonts, and a final polish pass. The new design is now live.

Part One

Your Original Requests

You gave us ten clear revision points, with one guiding idea: make OMJ feel premium — like Apple. Product first, minimal text, bold typography. Here is each request and exactly what we delivered.

1
Done

Hero Redesign — Product First

You asked Lead with oranges, juice, the machine, and the cup — not people. New headline: “Freshly Squeezed. Every Cup. Every Time.”

Delivered The hero is now a full-bleed, edge-to-edge product shot — your cup, front and center — with the new headline and the subhead “Made from whole oranges. No preservatives. No water. No concentrates.” No people. Just the product, the way you open Apple.com and see the iPhone.

2
Done

The Machine, Front and Center

You asked The vending machine — your competitive advantage — was buried near the bottom of the page. Move it high and show the squeezing process.

Delivered The machine now appears immediately after the hero, with a photo gallery that tells the story in order: oranges loaded, oranges squeezing, finished cup. Visitors see the process within the first scroll.

3
Done

Less Reading, More Scanning

You asked Long paragraphs that most visitors would never read.

Delivered Every long paragraph was broken into short, visual, scannable sections. Headlines carry the message; body text is one or two lines at most. The whole page reads in seconds, not minutes.

4
Done

Premium Icons Instead of Checkmarks

You asked Replace the generic green checkmarks with premium icons for your five value points.

Delivered Five icon tiles: 100% Fresh Oranges · No Water Added · No Added Preservatives · Served Fresh · Ready in Under 60 Seconds.

One deliberate upgrade: your sketch used emoji (🍊💧⚡). Emoji render differently on every phone and can look casual, so we designed custom, clean line icons in your brand colors instead — same idea, more premium. Note also that the value line reads “No Added Preservatives.” Happy to adjust either if you prefer.

5
Done

Show the Product Much More

You asked More of the product itself — vibrant juice, whole oranges, close-ups.

Delivered The product now appears throughout the page: the hero cup, the machine gallery, a dedicated cup section with close-up photography, and a cup carousel. The juice is the hero of the site.

6
Done

“Find a Machine” Section

You asked A prominent locations section — Greenhills, Eastwood, Uptown, Parqal — with Directions and Open in Maps buttons.

Delivered A full “Find a Machine” section with a card for each of the four locations, each with real photos, a short address, and one-tap Directions / Open in Maps buttons. We also added an interactive map (more on that below) and a “Find a Machine” button in the site header so it is reachable from anywhere.

7
Done

“Leave a Reply” Removed

You asked The blog comment form made the site look unfinished. Replace it with a clean contact option.

Delivered The comment form is gone. In its place, a clean contact path leads to a tidied-up Contact page with the form front and center.

8
Done

“Own an OMJ Machine”

You asked Reword the franchise section to “Own an OMJ Machine” with a “Join the Waitlist” button.

Delivered Live on the home page as “Own an OMJ Machine” — “Partner with the Philippines’ fastest-growing fresh-juice concept.” with a “Join the Waitlist” button. It captures interest without overcommitting.

9
Done

Trust Numbers

You asked People trust measurable facts — add real numbers.

Delivered A bold stat band in brand green: 500,000+ oranges squeezed · thousands of cups served · multiple Metro Manila locations · 100% fresh juice. Easy to update as the business grows.

10
Done

A Strong Closing CTA

You asked End the page with “Ready for Real Orange Juice?” and clear next steps.

Delivered The page closes with “Ready for Real Orange Juice?” and two buttons: Find a Machine and Contact Us — a confident finish that points visitors to the nearest cup.

Part Two

Before & After

The clearest way to see the change: your original live site, side by side with the new one. Scroll inside each frame to walk down the entire page.

Home Page

Before

The original home page — a people-holding-juice hero, the headline “Manila’s Best Freshly-Squeezed Orange Juice,” a “Leave a Reply” blog comment form, and an old contact block.

Scroll inside the frame to see the full page.

After

The new home page — product-first from the first pixel: full-bleed cup hero with the new headline, machine gallery, icon tiles, stat band, live map, and a strong close. The people-led hero and the “Leave a Reply” form are gone (requests 1 and 7).

Scroll inside the frame to see the full page.

Highlights of the New Home

A closer look at four of the new sections — each one answering a specific request from your brief.

New Section

The Machine

Real photos of the OMJ machines and the squeezing setup — now sitting high on the page, right after the hero.

↳ Request #2 — the machine is no longer hidden
New Section

By the Numbers

A bold trust band: 500,000+ oranges squeezed, thousands of cups served, multiple Metro Manila locations, and 100% fresh juice.

↳ Request #9 — add numbers
New Section

The Cup

New appetite-forward product photography — close-ups of the cup and vibrant juice that make you want to try it.

↳ Request #5 — show the product more
New Section

Find a Machine Near You

An interactive map with pin markers plus four location cards — Greenhills, Eastwood, Uptown BGC, and Parqal — each with Directions and Open in Maps.

↳ Request #6 — a prominent Find-a-Machine section

Contact Page

Before

The original contact page — a “We’d love to hear from you / Contact Us” block, the form, and an “Our Locations” list that still showed “San Antonio Plaza, Makati.”

Scroll inside the frame to see the full page.

After

The new contact page — one clean, centered form with your Instagram QR, and the old “San Antonio Plaza” location corrected to Eastwood.

About Page

The About page was refreshed in place rather than rebuilt — same familiar layout, better product imagery.

Current

The “about oh my juiceness” page as it stands today. The “Our Story” section now shows the real OMJ product cup (replacing the old bottle), and the “Interested to Franchise?” section uses a real photo of the OMJ machine in place of the previous rendered mockup.

Scroll inside the frame to see the full page.

On Mobile

Most of your visitors are on their phones, so the new design was built mobile-first.

After — Mobile

The new home page on a phone — the hero, gallery, icons, map, and CTA all adapt cleanly.

Scroll inside the frame to see the full page.

Part Three

Beyond the Brief

While rebuilding the site, we took the opportunity to fix and elevate things you didn’t ask for — all in service of that premium feel.

New Product Photography

A brand-new generated hero cup shot, a machine photo gallery, and a cup carousel — so the product looks as good online as it tastes in person.

Interactive Locations Map

A live Metro Manila map in the “Find a Machine” section. Tap a location card and the map glides to that machine — Greenhills, Eastwood, Uptown, and Parqal.

Fonts Fixed & Self-Hosted

Your brand fonts were silently failing to load from an old development domain. They are now hosted on your own site — loading reliably and fast, every visit.

Smooth Scroll Animations

Sections reveal gently as visitors scroll — subtle, Apple-style motion. Visitors who prefer reduced motion automatically get a still version.

Accessibility Built In

Keyboard focus states, a skip-to-content link, and descriptive alt text on images — the site works well for every visitor.

Final Polish Pass

Full-bleed edge-to-edge hero, stray pink borders (left behind by a reviews plugin) removed, duplicated sections on the contact page cleaned up, and the contact form centered.

“Find a Machine” in the Header

A dedicated button now lives in the site header, so finding your nearest machine is one tap away from any page.

Part Four

It’s Live

The new design is no longer on a staging link — it is the real website. The home page is live at ohmyjuiceness.com and the new contact page at /contact/. The old pages were retired, but we kept them safely recoverable in case anything ever needs to be referenced or restored.

Visit the Home Page Visit the Contact Page

Old versions are archived and recoverable — nothing was lost in the switch.

Part Five

A Few Things We Need From You

Nothing urgent — the site is live and looking great. But these six small items need your eyes or your input to fully close things out.

  • Display font license. The headline font (Augillion) is currently running on a demo license. To be fully squared away, we’ll need a purchased license — or we can swap in an alternative you love.
  • Eastwood photos. The best photos we have of the Eastwood machine are quite low-resolution, so they look a little softer than the other locations. A quick reshoot on any modern phone would fix this.
  • Exact machine spots. For each mall, could you tell us the floor and landmark? (e.g. “3F, near the cinema lobby.”) We’ll add it to the location cards so customers can walk straight to the machine.
  • The hero cup wordmark. Take one close look at the stylized “oh my juiceness” script on the hero cup — we want to be sure it can’t be misread at a glance. If you’d like, we can regenerate it.
  • “I want to order online” option. The contact form still offers this option. Since online ordering isn’t offered right now, let us know whether to keep or remove it.
  • About page photos. Per your direction, we’ll swap the bottle photo for a cup shot and the machine render for a real machine photo — the replacement images are ready. Just say go.

Prepared with care for Oh My Juiceness • July 2026 • ohmyjuiceness.com
